target.name: implementation
target.source_summary: agents/engineer.md (Must Not, Required Patterns, File Ownership) + templates/implementation-note.md + skills/implementation/enforce-thin-proxy.md, enforce-middleware-layers.md, implement-auth.md + skills/architecture/audit-state-boundaries.md, enrich-error-context.md. Grades code_module artifacts and the implementation_note together.

gates.description: Every file written falls within the role's owned_globs and the task's owned surfaces. A violation is the 'smuggled cleanup' / boundary-drift Must Not — binary.
The code loads in an isolate and answers one request without throwing at startup. Evidence over narration, minimum bar.

No catch-and-continue or fallback-value returns on critical paths: no `catch { return fallbackValue }`, no `catch { console.error(e) }` log-and-forget on payment/auth/state-mutating paths. Errors propagate to boundaries.
Background or async work carries status tracking: claim atomically, process with try/catch, mark complete or stuck. Work that completes leaving no trace fails.
PBKDF2 with 100,000 iterations via Web Crypto; no bcrypt/MD5/unsalted SHA-256; constant-time comparison for tokens.
gates.applies_when: auth, password, session, or token-comparison code is present in the change

dimensions.description: Proxies do exactly four things: extract, forward, log, return-with-enriched-error.

anchors.1: A proxy validates business rules, transforms domain data, or makes tier/quality decisions.
anchors.3: Four-step pattern present, but one proxy does extra work the others don't — the consistency principle is broken.
anchors.5: Every proxy is exactly extract/forward/log/return; error enrichment uses middleware context only; all proxies identical in shape.

dimensions.description: The diff is the minimal coherent implementation of the task.

anchors.1: Opportunistic redesign or unrelated refactors beyond the task boundary.
anchors.3: Within boundary, but bundles a drive-by cleanup or an abstraction nothing else uses yet.
anchors.5: Every changed file traceable to the task deliverable; no speculative structure.

dimensions.description: User-facing errors reach enrichment level 4: actionable.

anchors.3: Level 3: situation explained (current usage, limit, reset date) but no next step (upgrade URL, retry guidance).
anchors.5: Level 4 on every user-facing error: message + current usage stats + limits + actionable next steps, per the engineer's error-response pattern.

dimensions.description: Lifecycle state is authoritative, constrained, and queryable.

anchors.1: Authoritative state lives in memory or caches; statuses implicit or free-text.
anchors.3: Database-canonical, but at least one lifecycle state is unqueryable or a status column lacks CHECK constraints.
anchors.5: Explicit lifecycle states with CHECK constraints, timestamps, and indexes; 'what state is X in' is a single query; stuck/failed states exist.

dimensions.description: Root middleware handles session/context; API middleware handles subscription/usage/limits; handlers assume permission.

anchors.1: Auth checks inside handlers, or business logic inside middleware.
anchors.3: Layers exist but at least one concern sits in the wrong layer.
anchors.5: Concerns exactly placed; context.data shape complete; handlers contain zero re-verification.

dimensions.applies_when: the change touches middleware or request-context plumbing

dimensions.description: The note is honest and complete per the template — visible gaps score higher than hidden ones.

anchors.1: Sections empty or boastful ('everything works'); missing verification not mentioned.
anchors.3: Changes and files complete; assumptions or missing-verification omitted.
anchors.5: All sections substantive; assumptions explicit; missing verification listed visibly; risks named.

aggregation.gate_handling: Apply gate caps after normalization; lowest applicable cap wins. Dimensions out of scope via applies_when are not_scored and renormalized.

weight_rationale: thin_proxy_compliance is heaviest because proxy thickness is the repo's canonical structural failure — an entire skill (enforce-thin-proxy) exists solely to police it, and 'business logic creep' is named as the pattern that starts small and erodes the architecture.

unmeasurable_rules.rule: Verify before claiming (tests run, manual checks)
Implementation order: schema → utils → workers → middleware
Follow existing project patterns before inventing new ones
unmeasurable_rules.home: Partially in smallest_coherent_change; full check needs the target codebase as a secondary surface — provide repo context via needs_surface in v2.

known_good.summary: Login endpoint: thin proxy, layered middleware, level-4 errors, honest note.
known_good.content: functions/api/login.js: extracts {email,password}, forwards to workers/auth.js, logs to D1 usage (success+failure), returns response; on 429 adds {error, usage:{current,limit}, resetDate, upgradeUrl}. workers/auth.js: PBKDF2-100k via crypto.subtle, constant-time token compare, sessions row with CHECK status + expires_at index. Note: Changes listed per file; Assumptions: '30-day expiry assumed per session schema standard'; Verification: 'run_code: 200 valid / 401 invalid / 429 over-limit verified; NOT verified: concurrent session cap — flagged'; Risks: 'rate limiter uses fixed window'.

known_bad.summary: Thick proxy with silent fallback and a smuggled refactor.
known_bad.content: functions/api/login.js: validates email format inline, downgrades hash to SHA-256 'for speed' when PBKDF2 throws (catch { return sha256(pw) }), transforms the response shape, and also reformats functions/api/billing.js 'while in there'. Note: 'Implemented login. Everything works. Tests: will add later.'
